core_mechanism: critical slowing down of a mean-reverting stochastic relaxation process, whose restoring rate toward equilibrium weakens as a control parameter approaches a critical threshold, so that recovery time, temporal memory, autocorrelation and variance all grow as generic early-warning signatures of an approaching transition
brief_reason: The abstract builds a finite-memory multiplicative reversion (a mean-reverting stochastic process) and reads elevated regimes as showing stronger memory, rising autocorrelation, amplified variance and reduced resilience, i.e. critical slowing down expressed as a loss of restoring capacity near a critical point.
